text: Deep water running produces similar improvements in cardiorespiratory fitness compared to land-based running training.
Deep water running simulates land-based running patterns in an aquatic environment, making it a viable aerobic cross-training alternative.
The aquatic environment of deep water running provides advantages for high-intensity off-loaded exercise in populations that are weak, injured, or in pain.
Deep water running improves cardiorespiratory fitness compared to no-exercise control conditions.
Evidence for deep water running improving physical function and quality of life remains limited and inconclusive.
